contents.value:
```ignore
# comment text
```
A line starting with `#` is a comment. Comments are ignored by the pattern matcher and can be used to document patterns.

```ignore
*.log
```
Glob wildcard — matches any number of characters except the path separator `/`. Used in glob patterns like `*.log` or `src/*`.

```ignore
**/foo
abc/**
a/**/b
```
Recursive wildcard — matches zero or more directories. Can be used as a leading path (`**/foo`), trailing path (`abc/**`), or in the middle (`a/**/b`).

```ignore
file?.txt
```
Single character wildcard — matches exactly one character except the path separator `/`.

```ignore
*.log
!important.log
```
Negation prefix — negates a pattern so that a matching file excluded by a previous pattern becomes included again. Cannot re-include a file if a parent directory is excluded.

```ignore
/build
logs/
```
Directory separator/anchor — a leading `/` anchors to the repository root. A trailing `/` means the pattern matches only directories.

```ignore
*.[oa]
file[0-9].txt
[!abc]*.log
```
Character class — matches a single character from the set. Supports ranges (`[a-z]`) and negation (`[!abc]`).

```ignore
node_modules/
```
Node.js dependency directory containing installed npm/yarn/pnpm packages. Should almost always be ignored.

```ignore
.env
.env.local
.env.*.local
```
Environment variable configuration file, typically containing secrets and sensitive configuration. Should be ignored to prevent credential leaks.

```ignore
.DS_Store
```
macOS Finder metadata file that stores custom folder attributes (icon positions, view settings). Should be ignored cross-platform.

```ignore
Thumbs.db
```
Windows Explorer thumbnail cache database file. Should be ignored cross-platform.

```ignore
__pycache__/
```
Python bytecode cache directory created by the Python interpreter. Contains `.pyc` compiled files.

```ignore
dist/
```
Distribution/build output directory. Common in JavaScript, TypeScript, and many other project types.

```ignore
build/
```
Build output directory used by many build systems and frameworks (webpack, Create React App, etc.).

```ignore
coverage/
```
Test coverage report output directory generated by testing frameworks like Jest, Istanbul, nyc.

```ignore
.git
```
Git version control directory containing repository history and metadata. Commonly ignored in `.dockerignore` files.

```ignore
vendor/
```
Vendored dependency directory used in Go, PHP (Composer), and Ruby (Bundler) projects.

```ignore
.idea/
```
JetBrains IDE (IntelliJ, WebStorm, PyCharm, etc.) project configuration directory.

```ignore
.vscode/
```
Visual Studio Code workspace configuration directory. May contain shared settings or user-specific config.

```ignore
target/
```
Build output directory used by Rust (Cargo), Java (Maven), and other build systems.
